rulings.Case Title: ALBERT RICE VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 7, 2008, line 1. DEFENDANT SCOTT LABORATORIES, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. In moving for summary judgment, defendant contends that decedent was not exposed to any asbestos containing materials supplied by defendant. Although defendant attaches plaintiff's responses to defendant's special interrogatories, defendant relies only on plaintiff's testimony to shift the burden. This is insufficient under Weber v. John Crane, Inc. (2006) 143 Cal. App. 4th 1433. Defendant's objections to the Puccioni declaration are overruled. Plaintiff presents evidence that creates a triable issue of fact on the issue of exposure. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: DEBRA LEMMONS et al VS. JACKIE ROBINSON GARDEN APARTMENTS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 7, 2008, line 2. DEFENDANTS ROBERT FERRIS AND ROBERT BOCKELMAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is granted. The motion is timely and plaintiff's inability to present expert testimony on the legal malpractice claim is a new fact. Defendant shifted the burden of proof by demonstrating that expert testimony is necessary to prove the legal malpractice claim. The question of the application San Francisco Rent Ordinance is critical to plaintiff's claim and it is far from obvious (particularly to a lay person) that the subject unit is covered by the Rent Ordinance. Further, expert testimony would be necessary to explain why plaintiff did not waive the eviction claim in paragraph 8 of the stipulation and judgment. Plaintiff must address both of these issues before being able to assert that defendant gave erroneous advice as to the statute of limitations. Plaintiff's evidentiary objections are overruled. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: ARTHUR WEBB VS. WORKER'S COMPENSATION APPEALS BD. SAN FRANCISCO,CA

rulings.Case Title: LARRY TREFRY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 7, 2008, line 4. DEFENDANT DAP, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment and summary adjudication are denied. The issue raised is exposure and defendant has testified to the use of defendant's products. The Reed Declaration generally discusses defendant's products and asserts many did not contain asbestos but that does not demonstrate that plaintiff is unable to prove the DAP products he used contained asbestos to which plaintiff was exposed. The Reed Declaration lacks the requisite persuasiveness for it contains virtually no foundational facts for the statements made. Plaintiff also presents evidence creating a triable issue of fact based on the Declarations of Trefry, Dudick and the deposition testimony of Trefry. The DiCola objection is overruled. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: JANET PICKENS VS. BRENDA HUTCHINSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Requiring Pltf To Furnish Security Ccp 391.1; P/A; Request For Judicial Notice, Declaration ) Ccp 128.7
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 7, 2008, line 5. DEFENDANTS THE JOHN STEWART COMPANY AND BRENDA HUTCHERSON'S Motion For Order Requiring Plaintiff To Furnish Security CCP 391.1. The motion is granted. The Court has reviewed the complaint and the moving papers submitted by defendant pursuant to Moran v. Murtaugh Miller Meyer & Nelson, LLP (2007) 40 Cal. 4th 780. The Court finds that plaintiff has no probability of success in this action is required to post a bond of $500.00. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: BURLINGAME INVESTMENT CORPORATION et al VS. HERMAN KWAI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Hearing Re Preliminary Injunction And Temporary Restraining Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 7, 2008, line 14. ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION AND TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER. Hearing required. The parties are to address the following questions: 1. If the Barbados Court has issued a restraining order preventing defendants from holding themselves out as directors or officers of BIB, why should this Court fail to enforce that order? 2. Is this action necessary to maintain the status quo related the BIB's California activities and to enforce the Barbados Court order? 3. If Nat Chan is Jackie Lam's agent is there any other issue as to plaintiff's ability to bring this action? 4. Is Corporations Code § 709 limited to domestic corporations and foreign corporations where the appointment was made in California? If it does not apply to this dispute, does this Court have jurisdiction to decide the dispute? 5. Is Corporations Code § 709 the exclusive remedy to challenge the election of a director? 6. What is the basis for defendants' contention that the Barbados orders do not preclude defendants from exercising control over BIB? 7. Do defendants contend that Starble had a unilateral right to revoke the pledge to Chan Dong? What is the basis for that claim? 8. If the Court determines to issue a bond, what should be the amount of the bond and why? =(302/PJM/AL)
